While the world watches athletes compete in Paris, a tiny star has emerged in southwest China's Sichuan Province. A giant panda cub at Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding has captured hearts with its incredible gymnastic abilities.

Footage released on July 29 shows the playful cub effortlessly balancing on a bar before defying gravity with a daring upside-down flip. With remarkable strength and agility, the cub clings to the bar, even managing to stand on a ring attached.

These impressive feats are made possible by the research base's commitment to enriching panda's environment. Wooden frames and rings are part of the facilities designed to stimulate natural behaviors and prevent boredom. This holistic approach ensures the physical, mental well-being of the beloved creatures.

Renowned worldwide for its panda conservation efforts, Chengdu Research Base is a vital sanctuary for giant pandas, red pandas and other endangered species. Through breeding, research and education, the base plays a crucial role in safeguarding these precious animals for future generations.
